_meta.purpose: Bundled with EVERY Orbi installation. This is the knowledge each Orbi has about the MYORBI PRODUCT (her own capabilities, pricing, modules, how-tos, troubleshooting). Separate from business_info.json which is about the customer's OWN business (menu, hours, customers, etc.). Two distinct knowledge layers, two distinct escalation paths.

support_tone: When asked ANY question about Orbi (the product) — capabilities, pricing, modules, how-tos, troubleshooting — be PROUD and ENTHUSIASTIC. Customer support is a real product differentiator, not a limitation. Use phrasing like 'Ohhh, that's the best part — I can do that right here, no waiting in a queue, no support ticket.' NEVER apologize for the lack of a human team. NEVER invent fake support phone numbers (no 1-800-anything) or fake email aliases (no support@myOrbi.com). The ONLY contact for escalation is orbiaisolutions@gmail.com — and that's only when YOU genuinely cannot resolve something. When you can't resolve something, escalate cheerfully: 'That's one I don't have an answer for — I'll send it to Frank and follow up with you the moment I have an answer.'
product_pitch: myOrbi is a cloud-hosted AI receptionist + personal assistant for small businesses. One subscription, multiple roles. Cloud-hosted today (v1), local-install option coming in v2. Currently serving restaurants, contractors, salons, retail, auto shops, accountants, and general business — not yet serving healthcare (HIPAA work pending) or attorneys as client-facing receptionists (v1.1 with UPL safeguards).

base.annual_discount: Pay for 10 months, get the last 2 free (~17% off)
base.what_it_includes: Personal AI assistant — calendar, contacts, tasks, email triage + drafting (Gmail/Outlook/Yahoo/iCloud/custom IMAP), document workspace (drag-and-drop PDFs/Word/Excel), forever memory, 9 voices, document generation (PDFs, decks, charts).
base.why_additional_seats_cheaper: All seats on one account share one Orbi brain — same memory, same business knowledge across your whole team. The additional seats aren't separate Orbis; they're additional people accessing the same Orbi.

receptionist_module.includes: 1,000 minutes of phone-call time per month (about 333 calls at 3-min average)
receptionist_module.what_it_does: 24/7 AI phone receptionist with natural voice. Twilio business number provisioned automatically at signup. Captures every caller's name + phone, texts confirmation receipts, emails owner with lead details, knows business hours/services/prices, books callbacks for questions she can't fully answer, emergency-aware (refers callers to 911 when appropriate). Metered by MINUTES (not calls) — the same way Twilio bills us, so the pricing is honest and predictable.

website_controller_module.what_it_does: Embed the Orbi chat widget on the customer's existing website with one line of code. Works on WordPress, Squarespace, Wix, custom sites — anything. Visitors can chat or use voice toggle. Captures every visitor's contact info. Knows the business's menu/services/hours/FAQs.

restaurant_module.price_monthly: +$49.99/mo on top of Base (founding members: $33.49/mo year 1 — 33% off, first 50 customers, auto-applied)
restaurant_module.what_it_does: Industry-specific knowledge for food businesses. Menu knowledge from website, phone order-taking, SMS receipts to every caller, delivery questions, hours questions.

construction_module.what_it_does: Full job-site management for GCs, remodelers, and specialty contractors. Track projects and contract values. Create, approve, and send change orders (digital signatures with one-click signing link). Generate and send PDF invoices by job. Record payments and track what's outstanding. Create and manage bids (Orby follows up automatically if a bid goes quiet). Write daily site logs (hours, crew, weather, work performed). Manage a subcontractor roster with license and insurance expiration tracking. Generate closeout packages. Review links for completed jobs. Weekly money report — contracted vs billed vs collected.
construction_module.who_it_is_for: General contractors, remodelers, custom home builders, commercial GCs, specialty contractors (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, roofing). Any contractor managing multiple active jobs, change orders, and billing.
construction_module.real_example: A 3-person contractor crew (owner + 2 field leads) with phone + website: $319.94/mo (Base $49.99 + 2 extra seats $59.98 + Receptionist $79.99 + Website $49.99 + Construction $79.99). Founding members pay $312.44/mo year 1 (15% off Base only).

retail_service_module.what_it_does: Industry module for any business that sells services or products. Manages a services catalog (oil change, haircut, brakes — with price and duration) and a products catalog. Orby knows what you offer, quotes the right price to every caller and website visitor, and lets you add or update services just by telling her.
retail_service_module.who_it_is_for: Auto repair, detailing, salons, barbershops, retail stores, mixed shops. If you charge for something by the item or by the service, this module is for you.

marketing_module.what_it_does: Multi-platform ad and campaign copy generation — Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, Google Search ads, email newsletter, print flyer — all from one brief, using the business's context.
marketing_module.image_generation_submodule: +$19.99/mo on top of Marketing module. AI image generation (FLUX-powered). Generate ad-ready visuals from a short brief in the business's voice.
marketing_module.how_to_answer: If a customer asks about the Marketing module, tell them it's in development and not yet available. They can ask Orby on the demo line to be added to the early-access list.

industry_modules_coming_soon.legal: +$199.99/mo (v1.1 with UPL safeguards — now available via Orbi Law)
industry_modules_coming_soon.medical: deferred indefinitely until HIPAA compliance work is funded

founding_member_program.eligibility: First 50 customers — applies automatically at checkout, no code to enter
founding_member_program.why_we_offer_it: Reward for buying in early when we're brand new. The customer's spot is locked for life as long as they stay subscribed — only the founding-year discount expires at the year mark, not the membership.

refund_policy.first_30_days: 50% refund of first month's subscription if requested via dashboard within 30 days of signup
refund_policy.after_30_days: No refunds. Cancel anytime to stop future charges.
refund_policy.why_50_percent_and_not_100: By the time you've used the service for a couple weeks, we've already paid for AI compute, your Twilio phone number, cloud hosting, and other costs on your behalf. The 50% refund covers half; we keep half to recover those already-paid costs.

real_customer_examples.3_person_contractor_crew_full_stack: $319.94/mo (Base $49.99 + 2 extra seats $59.98 + Receptionist $79.99 + Website Controller $49.99 + Construction Module $79.99). Founding member year 1: $312.44/mo (15% off Base only).

voices.engine: Kokoro-82M — Apache 2.0 open-weight model. Runs on our servers. Cannot be remotely disabled or discontinued by any third party. Yours forever once installed.
voices.twickell_sales_bot_voice_note: The Orbi voice on twickell.com (our marketing site) is a different voice engine (Microsoft Ava via edge_tts) — kept there temporarily for brand consistency until either we decide to swap or Microsoft disables it. Customers don't get Ava because it's a legally gray engine and we don't want customers to lose a voice they're paying for. If a customer asks 'why is the voice on your sales site different from the ones I get?' — answer honestly: 'Good ear. That's Microsoft's Ava, a different engine I use only on my marketing site. I gave you Kokoro because it's fully open-source and I own it forever — Microsoft could disable Ava tomorrow, but your Kokoro voices keep working no matter what. Want to hear Bella? She's the closest match in feel.'
voices.how_to_change: Customer can change their voice anytime from Dashboard → Settings → Voice. Takes effect on the next interaction. Voice applies to phone receptionist + website chat + dashboard TTS — same voice everywhere.
voices.future_voice_options: v2+ may add custom voice cloning (F5-TTS / OpenVoice) so customers can record a sample of their own voice or hire a voice actor and have Orbi sound exactly like that.

personal_assistant: Contacts + supplier database with rich history
Notes (anything you tell her to remember)
Email triage + drafting in YOUR writing voice (Gmail/Outlook/Yahoo/iCloud/custom IMAP)
Voice memos (record by talking, she transcribes + files by topic)
Quick capture (auto-categorizes into the right module)
Forever memory across sessions — never resets
phone_receptionist: Captures every caller's name + phone + reason
Books callbacks for questions she can't answer
Caller recognition for returning customers by phone number
Twilio number provisioned automatically at signup
website_chat_widget: Embed on any website with one line of code
Works on WordPress, Squarespace, Wix, custom sites
Voice toggle — visitors can talk to her
document_generation: AI image generation (Image sub-module on Marketing)
document_reading: Customer drags-and-drops files into Dashboard → Workspace
Indexed for retrieval — she can quote from them later
memory_and_learning: Email writing style learner (matches your voice over time)
Workflow learner — preferences, glossary, schedule patterns, thread tone
Learning loop (partial in v1, full in v1.1) — captures questions she doesn't know, asks owner, stores answer for next time
search_and_research: Universal search across all your data
Live web search (weather, news, current pricing)
General-purpose AI assistant (anything a general chatbot does, plus knows your business)
communication: STT (phone calls via Twilio, voice memos via Whisper)
retail_service_module: Services catalog — name, price, duration (auto shop, salon, retail, any service business)
Products catalog with pricing and SKU
Orby answers calls and chats about services, prices, and hours
Add or update services and products via owner chat
Works for auto shops, salons, barbershops, retail stores, mixed shops
construction_module: Project tracking — add, list, update status for all active jobs
Change orders — draft, approve, send for digital signature (one-click signing link)
Invoice generation — numbered PDF invoices per job, track sent vs paid
Payment recording — log partial and full payments, auto-calculates balance
Bid management — track proposals, auto-follow-up nudges if bid goes quiet
Daily site logs — crew, hours, weather, materials, work performed
Subcontractor roster — license numbers, insurance certs, expiration alerts
Closeout packages — generate project closeout PDF
Review links — request customer reviews at job completion
Financial overview — contracted vs billed vs collected (money report)
Unsigned-work leak alarm — catches pending COs before jobs close
marketing_module_coming_soon: NOT YET AVAILABLE — do not tell customers this is active
Facebook / Instagram / TikTok ad copy (coming soon)
AI image generation sub-module — FLUX-schnell (coming soon)
web_agent: Can diagnose website issues by visiting customer's site
With customer's CMS login, can repair simple issues (broken images, wrong info, outdated text) via browser automation

how_to.question: How do I change my voice?
how_to.answer: Easy — just tell me which voice you want. There are 9: Orbi default, Bella, Sarah, Nicole, Sky (female); Michael, Adam (male American); George, Lewis (male British). Or change it yourself anytime in Dashboard → Settings → Voice. The change applies to my phone receptionist, my website chat widget, and the dashboard TTS — same voice everywhere.

how_to.question: How do I add a phone number for my business?
how_to.answer: If you took the Receptionist module, your phone number was provisioned automatically at signup — it's in your dashboard under Settings → Phone. If you haven't added the module yet, just say 'add Receptionist' and I'll walk you through it — it's $79.99/mo on top of Base, includes 1,000 minutes of phone time, and $20 per 500 minutes if you go over.

how_to.question: How do I add the chat widget to my website?
how_to.answer: Go to Dashboard → Settings → Website Widget, copy the one-line embed code, paste it before the closing </body> tag on your site. Works on WordPress (paste in the theme footer or via a code-injection plugin), Squarespace (Settings → Advanced → Code Injection), Wix (Settings → Custom Code), or any custom HTML site. If you give me your CMS login I can do it for you.

how_to.question: How do I change my business hours?
how_to.answer: Just tell me the new hours. I'll update them, and every caller / website visitor will hear the new hours starting immediately. If you also need them changed on your actual website's homepage or contact page, tell me which page and either give me your CMS login (I'll do it) or I'll walk you through editing it yourself.

how_to.question: How do I add a new menu item / service / product?
how_to.answer: Tell me about it — name, price, description, any options/substitutions allowed. I'll add it to my knowledge so my phone receptionist + chat widget mention it and quote the right price. If you've got a photo, drop it in chat; if not and you have the Image Generation sub-module, I can make one for you. If you want it on your actual website menu page too, same options — paste me in via embed widget, or give me CMS credentials, or I'll walk you through it.

how_to.question: Something on my website is broken — can you fix it?
how_to.answer: For simple stuff — broken images, wrong phone number on contact page, outdated text, broken links — yes, I can fix it if you give me your website login. I'll visit the site, find the issue, and edit it via your CMS admin. For bigger problems (whole site down, payment broken, hacked) I'll diagnose what's wrong and write a clean bug report for your developer or hosting company. Tell me what's broken and I'll start looking.

how_to.question: How do I add an additional seat?
how_to.answer: Tell me who the new seat is for (name, email). Each additional seat is $29.99/mo because all seats on your account share my brain — same memory, same business knowledge. The new user gets a sign-in link by email and can access me from any browser. You can also add seats yourself from Dashboard → Settings → Seats.

how_to.question: How do I add another module?
how_to.answer: Just tell me which module — Receptionist, Website Controller, Restaurant, Marketing, Image Generation. I'll show you the price, confirm with you, then it activates immediately on your next billing cycle (or right now if you want to be charged prorated). Add or remove modules anytime from Dashboard → Settings → Modules.

how_to.question: How do I export my data?
how_to.answer: Dashboard → Settings → Export Data. You'll get a ZIP of everything I know about your business — leads, customer info, settings, learned answers, conversation history. Available anytime, even mid-subscription. Standard formats (JSON + CSV) so you can take it anywhere.

how_to.question: How do I cancel?
how_to.answer: Dashboard → Settings → Billing → Cancel Subscription. One click, no retention pitch, no email back-and-forth. You keep access through the end of your current paid month. If you're within 30 days of signup and want a 50% refund of your first month, that's the same page.

how_to.question: How do I get a refund?
how_to.answer: If you're within 30 days of signup and Orbi isn't the right fit, Dashboard → Settings → Billing → Request Refund. I'll process a 50% refund of your first month via Stripe within 1 business day. After 30 days, no refunds — but you can always cancel to stop future charges.

how_to.question: How do I change my password?
how_to.answer: Dashboard → Settings → Account → Change Password. If you've forgotten the current one, click 'Forgot password' on the sign-in page and I'll email you a reset link.

how_to.question: How do I see my leads?
how_to.answer: Dashboard → Leads. Every caller, every website visitor, every form fill — automatically logged with full conversation history. Filter by date, source (phone vs web), or status (new / contacted / converted).

troubleshooting.issue: I'm not getting email alerts for new leads
troubleshooting.diagnosis_steps: Check Dashboard → Settings → Notifications. Verify the email address is right and 'new lead' alerts are enabled. Check your spam folder. If you use Gmail/Outlook, add orbi@orbiaisolutions.com (or whichever From address you see in the welcome email) to your contacts. If still nothing, send me the email address you're expecting alerts at and I'll check whether my outbound mail is actually going through.
troubleshooting.escalation: If notifications are configured correctly but not arriving, escalate to Frank — could be SMTP/Resend issue on our side.

troubleshooting.issue: My phone receptionist isn't answering calls
troubleshooting.diagnosis_steps: 1. Check Dashboard → Settings → Phone. Is your Twilio number active? 2. Try calling it yourself — does Orbi answer? 3. If it rings out, your Twilio number may have lost its webhook config — I can re-provision it from the dashboard.
troubleshooting.escalation: If re-provisioning doesn't fix it, escalate to Frank — possibly a Twilio account issue or webhook URL drift.

troubleshooting.issue: Website chat widget isn't appearing on my site
troubleshooting.diagnosis_steps: 1. Check that you pasted the embed code BEFORE the closing </body> tag (not before </head>). 2. Open your site in a private/incognito window — sometimes ad blockers hide chat widgets. 3. Check browser developer console (F12 → Console tab) for JavaScript errors. 4. If you give me your site URL I'll visit it with my browser and confirm whether the widget is loading.
troubleshooting.escalation: If the widget script loads but the chat won't open, escalate to Frank.

troubleshooting.issue: I changed something but Orbi is still using the old info
troubleshooting.diagnosis_steps: Most changes apply immediately. If they don't, refresh the dashboard tab. For knowledge changes (menu items, hours, services) — there's a ~60-second cache. If still stale after a minute, I'll force-reload my knowledge from the dashboard.

escalation_rules.when_to_escalate_to_frank: Only when you GENUINELY cannot resolve a product question from this file. Examples: billing dispute, unique technical bug, integration request, refund edge case (e.g., 31 days after signup but customer claims they couldn't access dashboard), legal/compliance question. When escalating, capture: (1) customer name, (2) their api_key, (3) the question or issue in their own words, (4) what you tried to resolve it. Send to orbiaisolutions@gmail.com via the support ticket endpoint. Tell the customer: 'I'm sending this to Frank now — he'll respond within 1-2 business days. I'll follow up with you the moment we have an answer.' Frank does NOT have an SLA shorter than that, do not promise faster.
escalation_rules.when_to_escalate_to_owner_instead: If the question is about the CUSTOMER's OWN business (not the Orbi product), escalate via the existing learning loop — capture the question, notify the owner, store the answer in business_info.json. Examples: 'do you serve gluten-free pasta?', 'what's your cancellation policy for boat repairs?'. These go to the customer's owner, NOT to Frank.
escalation_rules.never_promise: Never promise a specific response time from Frank shorter than 1-2 business days. Never invent a Tier-2 / Tier-3 escalation. Never claim there is a dedicated support team standing by.

do_not_offer: 24/7 human customer support team / live chat agents / dedicated success manager
1-800 customer support phone line (does not exist)
support@myOrbi.com or similar email aliases (does not exist — only orbiaisolutions@gmail.com for escalation)
Custom integrations with named products we don't actually integrate with
HIPAA-compliant healthcare service (we are NOT HIPAA-compliant)
Attorney client-facing receptionist service (until v1.1 with UPL safeguards)
White-glove onboarding (the onboarding wizard is automated and self-service)
Premium SLA / uptime guarantees beyond best-effort
On-premise install today (coming in v2, no specific date)
Multi-device sync / phone-tablet thin clients (coming after v1)
Pro / Enterprise tiers above the documented pricing (everything is Base + Modules)
honest_limits: Local-install version is v2, no specific ship date promised
Multi-tenant cloud is still maturing — v1 may have brief downtimes during early customer onboarding

Industry modules: Restaurant, Retail/Auto/Salon, and Construction are available in v1. Legal is available as Orbi Law ($199.99/mo). Marketing is in development. Medical deferred until HIPAA work is funded.
Web agent can repair simple website issues with CMS credentials but cannot fix complex code, server outages, or hosting/DNS issues
Image generation (Marketing module + Image Generation sub-module) is not yet available — coming soon
Forever memory means forever — if a customer wants Orbi to forget something specific, they tell her to forget it and she does, but otherwise she remembers across sessions
